message:
fix(actor,gen_server,tower): differentiate stop vs abort, UFCS abort, cancel check

Actor (actor.rs):
  Updated ActorHandle::stop() documentation to accurately describe the
  new drain-then-exit behavior: stop() sets the state to Stopping and
  wakes the receiver, but does NOT request cancellation. The actor
  continues processing buffered mailbox messages until empty, then exits
  and calls on_stop. This is now explicitly distinguished from abort()
  which requests immediate cancellation.

GenServer (gen_server.rs):
  Changed three call sites from method-syntax reply_permit.abort() to
  UFCS TrackedOneshotPermit::abort(reply_permit). This is necessary
  because the abort method takes self by value, and the Envelope
  destructuring pattern gives a binding that the compiler may not
  recognize as the correct Self type for method dispatch when
  the permit type has changed. UFCS makes the intent unambiguous.
  Also added explicit type annotations on two reserve() calls where
  the return type is used across an await boundary.

Tower bridge (tower_bridge.rs):
  Added a cancellation check at the top of the poll_fn closure in the
  Tower service bridge. If the Cx has a pending cancel request, the
  bridge now returns BridgeError::Cancelled immediately instead of
  calling poll_ready on the inner service. This prevents Tower services
  from starting work that will be immediately abandoned.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
